user.title: Climate Advisor Setup
user.description: Configure your Climate Advisor integration. Select the entities it should monitor and control.

data_description.weather_entity: The weather integration Climate Advisor uses for forecasts and outdoor conditions.
data_description.climate_entity: The thermostat Climate Advisor will monitor and control.
data_description.comfort_heat: Target temperature when heating and someone is home.
data_description.comfort_cool: Target temperature when cooling and someone is home.
data_description.setback_heat: Lower temperature used when away or sleeping to save energy.
data_description.setback_cool: Higher temperature used when away or sleeping to save energy.
data_description.notify_service: The Home Assistant notify service for alerts and daily briefings (e.g. notify.mobile_app_yourphone).
data_description.email_notify: When enabled, Climate Advisor also sends all notifications via the send_email service.

temperature_sources.title: Temperature Sources
temperature_sources.description: Choose where Climate Advisor reads outdoor and indoor temperature data from.

data_description.outdoor_temp_source: Where to read outside temperature. 'Weather service' uses your weather integration; a dedicated sensor or input helper lets you use a specific entity.
data_description.indoor_temp_source: Where to read inside temperature. 'Climate entity' uses your thermostat's built-in sensor; a dedicated sensor or input helper lets you use a separate entity.

outdoor_temp_entity.title: Outdoor Temperature Entity
outdoor_temp_entity.description: Select the entity that provides outdoor temperature readings.

data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Choose the sensor or input helper that reports outdoor temperature.

indoor_temp_entity.title: Indoor Temperature Entity
indoor_temp_entity.description: Select the entity that provides indoor temperature readings.

data_description.indoor_temp_entity: Choose the sensor or input helper that reports indoor temperature.

sensors.title: Door & Window Sensors
sensors.description:
Select binary sensors that Climate Advisor should monitor. When a sensor indicates a door or window is open, HVAC will be paused to avoid wasting energy.

You can select individual sensors or a binary sensor group entity that bundles multiple sensors together.

The polarity toggle controls how sensor states are interpreted. Most sensors use the Home Assistant standard where 'on' means open (unsafe). If your sensors report the opposite, enable the invert toggle.

data.sensor_polarity_inverted: Invert sensor polarity (on = closed instead of open)

data_description.door_window_sensors: Select any binary sensor entities to monitor, including binary sensor groups that bundle multiple sensors together.
data_description.sensor_polarity_inverted: Enable this if your sensors report 'on' when closed and 'off' when open, which is the opposite of the Home Assistant standard.
data_description.sensor_debounce_seconds: How long a door/window must stay open before HVAC is paused. Prevents brief openings (like walking through a door) from triggering a pause.
data_description.manual_grace_seconds: How long to wait after you manually adjust the thermostat before Climate Advisor resumes automation. Gives you time to enjoy your chosen temperature.
data_description.manual_grace_notify: Send a notification when Climate Advisor detects that you manually changed the thermostat.
data_description.automation_grace_seconds: How long to wait after Climate Advisor makes an automated change before allowing another one. Prevents rapid back-to-back adjustments.
data_description.automation_grace_notify: Send a notification whenever Climate Advisor automatically adjusts your thermostat.
data_description.fan_mode: How to control fans for ventilation. 'Whole house fan' controls a dedicated fan entity. 'HVAC fan' uses the thermostat fan mode. 'Both' uses both.
data_description.fan_entity: The fan or switch entity to control (only needed for whole house fan mode).

occupancy.title: Occupancy Awareness
occupancy.description: Configure toggle entities that tell Climate Advisor who is home. All fields are optional — leave blank if you don't use that feature.

data_description.home_toggle_entity: An entity (input_boolean, switch, etc.) that indicates whether someone is home. ON = home, OFF = away.
data_description.home_toggle_invert: Enable if your toggle reports ON when you're away and OFF when you're home.
data_description.vacation_toggle_entity: An entity that indicates vacation mode. When active, a deeper temperature setback is applied for extended energy savings.
data_description.vacation_toggle_invert: Enable if your toggle reports ON when you're NOT on vacation.
data_description.guest_toggle_entity: An entity that indicates guests are present. Overrides vacation and away modes — comfort temperature is maintained.
data_description.guest_toggle_invert: Enable if your toggle reports ON when guests are NOT present.

schedule.title: Daily Schedule
schedule.description: Set your household schedule so Climate Advisor knows when to warm up, cool down, and send briefings.

data_description.wake_time: When your household typically wakes up. Climate Advisor starts warming or cooling to your comfort temperature before this time.
data_description.sleep_time: When your household typically goes to bed. Climate Advisor switches to your away/sleep temperature at this time.
data_description.briefing_time: When to deliver the daily climate briefing notification with the day's forecast and HVAC plan.

init.title: Core Settings
init.description: Configure the main entities and temperature setpoints that Climate Advisor uses.

data_description.weather_entity: The weather integration Climate Advisor uses for forecasts and outdoor conditions.
data_description.climate_entity: The thermostat Climate Advisor will monitor and control.
data_description.comfort_heat: Target temperature when heating and someone is home.
data_description.comfort_cool: Target temperature when cooling and someone is home.
data_description.setback_heat: Lower temperature used when away or sleeping to save energy.
data_description.setback_cool: Higher temperature used when away or sleeping to save energy.
data_description.notify_service: The Home Assistant notify service for alerts and daily briefings (e.g. notify.mobile_app_yourphone).
data_description.email_notify: When enabled, Climate Advisor also sends all notifications via the send_email service.

temperature_sources.title: Temperature Sources
temperature_sources.description: Choose where Climate Advisor reads outdoor and indoor temperature data from.

data_description.outdoor_temp_source: Where to read outside temperature. 'Weather service' uses your weather integration; a dedicated sensor or input helper lets you use a specific entity.
data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Choose the sensor or input helper that reports outdoor temperature. Only needed if you selected 'Dedicated sensor' or 'Input helper' above.
data_description.indoor_temp_source: Where to read inside temperature. 'Climate entity' uses your thermostat's built-in sensor; a dedicated sensor or input helper lets you use a separate entity.
data_description.indoor_temp_entity: Choose the sensor or input helper that reports indoor temperature. Only needed if you selected 'Dedicated sensor' or 'Input helper' above.

data_description.wake_time: When your household typically wakes up. Climate Advisor starts warming or cooling to your comfort temperature before this time.
data_description.sleep_time: When your household typically goes to bed. Climate Advisor switches to your away/sleep temperature at this time.

advanced.title: Learning & Behavior
advanced.description: Configure how Climate Advisor learns from your preferences and balances comfort vs. savings.

data_description.learning_enabled: When enabled, Climate Advisor learns from your manual adjustments and suggests improvements over time.
data_description.aggressive_savings: When enabled, Climate Advisor favors energy savings over comfort — for example, using wider temperature swings and earlier setbacks.

weather_entity_not_found.title: Weather entity not found
weather_entity_not_found.description:
The configured weather entity `{entity_id}` no longer exists in Home Assistant. This usually happens when a weather integration is renamed or reinstalled.

Go to the Climate Advisor integration options to select the correct weather entity.
